title: [WIP] Add documentation on the setting up dnsname resolution
body:
Fixes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/12074

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

Please put your overall PR description here
-->

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@mheon @baude @Luap99 Please verify that this is correct.  Once we have the english correct I will copy this section to other man pages podman create and podman network create.
Do we need this? We're just about to replace this on main by integrating it into Podman.

I don't believe it will be found in podman network create. Users are going to look in podman network create.

I don't mind making this smaller in podman run and podman create and then putting more documentation in podman network create. Something like:

```
## Name resolution

See podman network create for information on adding DNS name resolution to your container networks.
```

This is not going away in RHEL for quite some time, so I would like to get it documented somewhere.  I know netavark is coming, but right now dns name configuration is a black ark that I don't find documented anywhere a normal human would look.
But the upstream documentation and manpages won't be shipped in RHEL, and by the time we do a release with the changes you're making they'll be obsolete.

I would like to see the documentation back ported to released versions if needed.  I am less confident in us replacing CNI right a way. and my understanding was the CNI was going to be continued to be supported for a while.
CNI will remain, dnsname will not. We're integrating it into Podman.
